text:
Once there was a wise old owl who lived deep in the Whispering Woods. This owl didn’t want people coming near because they were noisy. He made his home high in the tallest oak tree.

At first, it seemed like harmony. Then came the foxes.

The foxes liked to chat with the old owl. They told stories. Sometimes the stories were fun. Sometimes the stories were silly. But the foxes were persistent.

After a week, the foxes moved in. They brought their kits.

The kits liked to visit the old owl. They’d talk for ages. The young foxes loved owl tales.

One autumn, when all leaves had fallen, the young foxes discovered the ancient home.

“What stories do you have?”

The oldest fox tilted his head. “Oh lots,” he laughed. “Once there was a great goose. He could fly backwards!”

“Tell us,” begged the kits.

“The goose? He’d talk forever if you let him,” chuckled the fox
